title: Pharmacy Services Program Learning Outcomes
content:
Pharmacy Services Program Learning Outcomes

Program Learning Outcomes

1. Program Learning Outcomes: With the successful completion of this program, students will be able to:

2. Acquire kowledge about collecting informations about Pharmacy services and the ability to use the theoretical and practical knowledge about Pharmacy Services.

3. Knows how to use the books, tools and new technologies to receive the updated information about the Pharmacy Services.

4. The student manages a duty and also would be able to solve the problems under the supervision of pharmacist by using the knowledge about his field at a basic level.

5. The student possesses the consciousness of learning main health procedures about prescribed medications and developing skills according to the expectations of the system.

6. The student who graduated from this programme would be able to work in group studies as a group member by the conciousness of collective studying.

7. The student is sensitive to the events related to his field of study in the world and monitors the progresses permanently.

8. Keeps records on pharmacy services under the responsibility of the pharmacist , uses information and communication technologies necessary for this purpose.

9. The student has sufficient consciousness about public health, environmental protection and work safety issues.

10. The student supports the social environment projects and activities with a sense of social responsibility and vocational skills.

11. The student complies with and contributes to quality management and processes.

12. The student has the knowledge of structure and physiology of healthy and sick patients thus he/she possesses the consciousness of the positive effects on comunity by rationalist drug using.

13. The student exemplifies the community with sensitivity about health,personal care,his/her demeanor, attitude and behavior.

14. The student who graduated from this programme use Turkish Language properly, follows the principles of Atatürk and would be able to speak at least one foreign language.
